name: developer
description: Unified development experience for building, evolving, and shipping agent-index collections — from first idea through marketplace submission. v1.8.0: `release` task (1.0.0 → 1.1.0) now generates BOTH halves of the mature two-phase process — an idempotent build-and-prep script (adapter `npm test` with module-resolution exit-classification, native `npm run build` + `exec_bundle_checksum`/`bundle_built_at` stamp, `api/*-manifest.json` restamp, resource-listings restamp, fail-closed version-consistency gate) AND the gated push script (changelog date stamp, mandatory preflight, per-repo commit→push→tag in code-first/listings-last order, dist handoff). Codifies the exact flow validated in C.1.3.5; the version-consistency gate now asserts manifest alignment at the EARLIEST phase (a gate that checks only collection.json shipped core 3.22.5's 20 manifests a version behind). release-checklist gains the matching lesson. v1.7.1: release-checklist Lesson added (C.1.3.3) — a release script's adapter-test step must CLASSIFY the exit, treating `node --test` module-resolution failures (the `file:../agent-index-filesystem` dep not linking on Windows/Git Bash → `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` before any assertion) as an advisory skip rather than a failure; the bundle build + `exec_bundle_checksum` match is the authoritative gate. Prevents the cry-wolf pattern where an operator could 'continue anyway' past a real failure. v1.7.0: ship-side codification — new `release` task generates a host-native, preflight-gated, version-gated release push script (code-repos-first/listings-last ordering, per-repo commit→push→tag v<version>, never-move-a-published-tag, backend-distribution publish handoff) — the ship-side counterpart to core's clone-script-generator, turning the hand-copied push-*.sh tribal knowledge into a generated, repeatable step; develop's lifecycle Release stage now names tagging + /shared/dist/ publish and routes to @ai:release; a standalone release-checklist reference resolves the lifecycle's dangling reference; preflight adds a release-readiness note. v1.5.0: access-model currency — develop teaches the three access patterns (open-commons / owned-content / two-tier hybrid) with the verified-outcome grant gate, pointer conventions, org sharing vocabulary, collaborative-acls.json scaffolding and id:-anchor guidance; preflight gains Step 7.5 Access-Model Consistency (errors on shared writes without ACL provisioning and on retired constructs); optimize Step 7 example modernized to the pointer-index model. Earlier: see CHANGELOG.
author: agent-index
